model.label: MiMo-V2-Pro
model.description: MiMo • The flagship foundation for the agentic era, built for complex, long-horizon, multi-step agentic tasks.

pick.rationale: Illinois holds a significant analytical edge with KenPom rank 7 and the nation's #1 adjusted offensive efficiency, while North Carolina ranks 29th and is missing star freshman Caleb Wilson for the season. The Illini's elite offensive firepower should exploit UNC's diminished roster, though the Tar Heels have shown resilience going 5-3 without Wilson. Illinois's superior talent depth and matchup advantages make them the clear favorite in this Round of 32 contest.

reasoningStep.title: North Carolina vs Illinois Round of 32 Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Analyzing the matchup between North Carolina and Illinois, considering team ratings, injuries, and matchup dynamics.
reasoningStep.evidence: Illinois has KenPom rank 7 and Torvik rank 6 with the nation's #1 adjusted offensive efficiency (adj_o_rank 1). North Carolina has KenPom rank 29 and Torvik rank 27 with offensive efficiency rank 33. UNC is missing star freshman Caleb Wilson (season-ending thumb injury), going 5-3 without him. Illinois has minor injury concerns with Keaton Wagler (shoulder) but appears healthy overall. Both teams have 24-8 records, but Illinois's superior offensive firepower and UNC's key absence create a clear advantage for the Illini.
